title: The Decoding Problem
concept: Generating text requires selecting words step-by-step; greedy selection is fast but sub-optimal, while checking all combinations is too slow.

exercises.prompt: How does a modern AI model-based AIs typically generate a typically generate a sentence?

options.text: All at once in a single flash
One word at a time, step-by-step
By translating from another language first

exercises.explain: AI models generate text step-by-step, predicting and choosing one word after another.

cards.title: Predicting the Future
cards.body: When you type on your phone, it predicts the next word. But how does an AI write a whole paragraph that actually makes sense? It's harder than it looks, because one bad word choice early on can ruin the entire story!

cards.title: What is the Decoding Problem?
cards.body: The Decoding Problem is the challenge of selecting the best sequence of words from millions of possible combinations. Because an AI predicts the next word based on probability, it needs a smart strategy to build a complete, natural-sounding sentence without getting stuck or taking forever.

cards.title: The Train Track Trap
cards.body: Imagine building a sentence like placing train tracks. If you only look at the immediate next curve (greedy search), you might end up crashing into a mountain. If you try to map out every possible track route in the world first, you will run out of time before the train even starts!

cards.title: Greedy vs. Smart Planning
cards.body: If an AI starts a sentence with 'I want to eat...', a greedy generator might instantly pick 'ice' because it is highly likely on its own. But if it looked ahead just one step, it might realize 'a healthy salad' makes much more sense for the current conversation!

cards.title: Myth: AI Thinks in Paragraphs
cards.body: Many people think AI models write a sentence by planning it out all at once like a human writer. In reality, they generate text one single word at a time, constantly guessing what should come next based on what they just wrote.

cards.title: Speed vs. Quality
cards.body: Depending on how we choose words, we either get fast but low-quality text, or slow but high-quality text. Finding the balance is the core of the decoding problem.

left.text: Super fast, but gets stuck in repetitive loops and makes silly mistakes.

right.text: Perfect sentences, but takes hours just to generate a single paragraph.

exercises.prompt: Why can't an AI just calculate and compare every single possible sentence before printing one?

options.text: It takes too much time and computing power.
Computers are not allowed to look at multiple words.
The AI will run out of memory after three words.
It always results in grammatical errors.

exercises.explain: Checking every single combination of words is mathematically impossible for long sentences because the number of possibilities grows too fast.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Greedy selection is computationally fast but can lead to sub-optimal sentences.
exercises.explain: Greedy search is very fast because it only makes one quick decision per word, even though the quality might not be the highest.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe this fast but limited word selection strategy.
exercises.sentence: Selecting only the immediate best next word is known as a [blank] search.
exercises.explain: A greedy search strategy is fast because it only looks at the very next word, but it often misses the best overall sentence because it doesn't plan ahead.

exercises.prompt: Which AI generation shows the error of getting stuck in a repetitive loop due to a bad decoding strategy?

options.text: AI A: "The weather today is sunny, so we should go play outside."
AI B: "The weather today is sunny and sunny and sunny and sunny."
AI C: "It is sunny today, let's take a walk to the park."

exercises.explain: AI output B shows typical 'greedy search' repetitive behavior, getting stuck in a loop because it only selects the most common next word without looking at the bigger picture.

exercises.prompt: Why is generating text described as a 'decoding problem' for AI?

options.text: Computers can easily check every word combination in seconds.
Choosing only the single best next word always makes perfect sentences.
Greedy search is very fast, but it often misses the best overall sentence.
AI models write sentences backwards to save time.

exercises.explain: Evaluating every single combination of words takes an impossible amount of time, while picking only the single best next word can lead to repetitive or nonsensical sentences. AI models must find a balance between speed and quality.
